title: pedicularis coppeyi
intro: Pedicularis coppeyi, also known as Coppey's lousewort, is a species of flowering plant in the balm family, native to New Zealand. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pedicularis coppeyi include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its possible use in treating skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve infusions or teas, made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.
